retrieval_text: Cinnamon is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 521 works and 2615 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Cinnamon, Cinnamon Flower. Common plant-part co-mentions include Bark, Root, Seed, Gum, Leaf, Flower. Common preparation co-mentions include Oil, Powder, Tincture, Syrup, Extract, Pill. Common condition co-mentions include Cold or catarrh, Diarrhoea or looseness, Vomiting or nausea, Fever, Dysentery, Cough. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Child-use caution, Bleeding caution, Allergy or sensitivity caution, Toxicity watch. Representative source works include The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J. Rennie.; The dispensatory of the United States of America / by Geo. B. Wood and Franklin Bache.; Gray's supplement to the pharmacopoeia : being a concise but comprehensive dispensatory and manual of facts and formulae, for the chemist and druggist and medical practitioner / by Theophilus Redwood.; Gray's supplement to the Pharmacopoeia : being a concise but comprehensive dispensatory and manual of facts and formulae, for the chemist and druggist and medical practitioner / by Theophilus Redwood.. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
